edges.description: Aberrant lymphatic structures infiltrate bone and adjacent soft tissue.
Bone is resorbed and structurally weakened over time.
Thoracic extension of abnormal lymphatic tissue can disrupt pleural lymphatic drainage.
Active osteolysis commonly causes localized pain.
Thoracic lymphatic disruption can produce chylous pleural effusions.

nodes.description: Dysregulated lymphatic endothelial growth drives expansion of abnormal lymphatic channels within and around bone.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Gorham-Stout disease (GSD) is a rare complex lymphatic malformation.

nodes.description: Bisphosphonates are commonly used to reduce osteolysis and skeletal complications in GSD.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Surgery (66.9%) and bisphosphonates (56.9%) are still the mainstream treatment methods, with a total of 33 (18.2%) patients receiving sirolimus.

nodes.description: Thoracic lymphatic involvement can lead to recurrent or persistent chylothorax.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Pleural effusion was identified as a risk factor for patient mortality (P < 0.05).

nodes.description: Thoracic lymphatic disruption can lead to recurrent or persistent chylothorax.
Pathologic lymphatic tissue progressively replaces normal bone architecture.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Gorham-Stout disease (GSD) is a rare complex lymphatic malformation.

nodes.description: Progressive disappearing bone lesions are the defining feature of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: It commonly manifests as multiple osteolysis of the axial bone, with pain being the most common symptom.

nodes.description: Progressive osteolysis causes pain, deformity, fracture risk, and loss of function.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: It commonly manifests as multiple osteolysis of the axial bone, with pain being the most common symptom.

nodes.description: mTOR inhibition with sirolimus is used in progressive disease to suppress lymphatic proliferation and reduce complications.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Surgery (66.9%) and bisphosphonates (56.9%) are still the mainstream treatment methods, with a total of 33 (18.2%) patients receiving sirolimus.

nodes.description: Active osteolysis often produces persistent pain in the involved bone.
Selected patients require stabilization, pleural procedures, or resection for site-specific complications.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Surgery (66.9%) and bisphosphonates (56.9%) are still the mainstream treatment methods, with a total of 33 (18.2%) patients receiving sirolimus.

nodes.description: Thoracic extension of abnormal lymphatic tissue can disrupt pleural drainage and predispose to pleural effusion.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features and current management experience in Gorham-Stout disease: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Pleural effusion was identified as a risk factor for patient mortality (P < 0.05).
